questions.stem: An organization has decided to move forward with a new strategic initiative. What should happen next to formally allow the project work to begin?
choices: Approve a document authorizing the project
questions.explanation: A project must be formally authorized before work begins. This authorization establishes the project and gives the project manager authority.

questions.stem: During project execution, the team realizes progress is slower than expected compared to the approved plan. Which activity should occur first?
choices: Measure the performance against the plan
questions.explanation: Performance must first be measured against the plan to understand variance before decisions about corrective actions are made.

questions.stem: A project team is discussing possible ways to complete a deliverable and evaluating different approaches before work begins. Which process group does this represent?
questions.explanation: Evaluating alternatives and determining how work will be performed are planning activities.

questions.stem: A project phase has completed and the deliverables were accepted. What should the project manager do next?
choices: Perform formal closure activities for the phase
Archive documents at the end of the project only
questions.explanation: Each phase requires closure activities such as documenting lessons learned and archiving project information.

questions.stem: Which statement best describes how process groups apply to projects?
choices: Only the project itself uses the five process groups
Only large projects require all process groups
Every phase and the overall project go through all process groups
Only planning and execution are required for most projects
questions.explanation: Every project and each phase within the project passes through all five process groups.

questions.stem: A project manager is ensuring that all parts of the project work together properly and that changes are evaluated for overall project impact. What area of management is primarily being applied?
questions.explanation: Integration management ensures that different parts of the project are coordinated and controlled as a whole.

questions.stem: The project manager is working to define exactly what work must be completed and what work is excluded from the project. Which area is the focus?
questions.explanation: Scope management defines what work is included in the project and what is outside the project boundaries.

questions.stem: Which area focuses on determining when project activities should occur and how long they will take?
questions.explanation: Schedule management addresses activity sequencing, duration estimation, and timeline development.

questions.stem: A project manager must request resources from department managers and has little authority over the team members performing the work. Which organizational structure is most likely?
questions.explanation: In a functional structure, department managers control resources and the project manager has limited authority.

questions.stem: A project manager and a department manager both share authority over team members. What organizational structure does this describe?
questions.explanation: Matrix organizations share authority between functional managers and project managers.

questions.stem: In which environment does the project manager typically have the greatest authority over project resources and decisions?
questions.explanation: Projectized organizations give the project manager full authority over the project and team.

questions.stem: Team members complain they are receiving conflicting priorities from two different managers. What type of structure is most likely causing this?
questions.explanation: Matrix organizations create dual reporting relationships which can cause conflicting priorities.

questions.stem: Which advantage is commonly associated with a projectized structure?
choices: Strong project focus and clear leadership
questions.explanation: Projectized structures create strong project focus and clear authority for the project manager.

questions.stem: Who is primarily accountable for ensuring the project meets its objectives within the approved boundaries?
questions.explanation: The project manager is responsible for delivering the project within the approved scope, schedule, cost, and quality targets.

questions.stem: Which responsibility is most critical for the project manager during planning?
questions.explanation: Project managers must establish achievable boundaries and ensure objectives are realistic.

questions.stem: A stakeholder suggests adding new functionality to the project deliverable. What should the project manager do first?
choices: Recognize it as a potential change
questions.explanation: Any request that modifies project work must first be recognized as a change before evaluation.

questions.stem: After identifying a change request, what should the project manager do next?
questions.explanation: Change requests must be analyzed to understand impacts before decisions are made.

questions.stem: An organization encourages open discussion and collaboration across teams. How will this most likely affect project change initiatives?
choices: Change will be easier to implement
Projects will take longer to complete
questions.explanation: Collaborative cultures generally support change and make adoption easier.

questions.stem: Which leadership approach is most likely to increase resistance to change?
questions.explanation: Limited communication and rigid leadership often cause resistance to change.

questions.stem: An organization has a rigid hierarchy with long reporting chains. What challenge is most likely for project communication?
questions.explanation: Hierarchical structures often slow communication due to multiple approval levels.

questions.stem: A project manager must ensure the project stays within scope, schedule, cost, and quality targets. What are these collectively called?
questions.explanation: Baselines represent the approved targets that the project must meet.

questions.stem: Which process group involves coordinating people and resources to carry out the project plan?
questions.explanation: Executing is where the project team performs the work defined in the plan.

questions.stem: What is the main goal of the monitoring activities during a project?
choices: Ensure work follows the approved plan
questions.explanation: Monitoring focuses on comparing performance against the plan and correcting deviations.

questions.stem: Which activity is commonly performed during project closure?
questions.explanation: Closure includes documenting lessons learned and archiving project records.
